title: Dysoxylum gotadhora
taxonomy: Dysoxylum gotadhora is classified in the family Meliaceae. This species belongs to the genus Dysoxylum, which is a group of flowering plants commonly found in tropical regions. The plant is further classified under the order Sapindales and the class Magnoliopsida. Dysoxylum gotadhora is native to the Himalayan region and is known for its medicinal properties. Its classification as a flowering plant is also reflected in its botanical characteristics, including its typical flower structure and leaf arrangement.
uses: Dysoxylum gotadhora, a plant native to the Western Ghats, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its medicinal uses include treating fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. The plant's extracts have been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In addition, it is used to treat various skin conditions, such as eczema and dermatitis. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential benefits of Dysoxylum gotadhora in modern medicine.

intro: Dysoxylum gotadhora, also known as Gotadhora or Gotu Kola, is a plant species belonging to the family Meliaceae, commonly found in the Himalayan and Eastern Asian regions. The plant is valued for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-cancer properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis, improve skin health, and prevent certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of Dysoxylum gotadhora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Dysoxylum gotadhora is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into capsules and tinctures, which are commonly used to promote overall well-being and reduce inflammation.
